task_completion.description: Open the human mitochondrial reference genome file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A bioinformatics GUI tool (e.g., UGENE) showing the 'human_mtDNA.gb' file loaded, with sequence or annotations visible.

task_completion.description: Identify and extract the 22 tRNA sequences from the genome.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Selection, filtering, or extraction dialog showing the tRNA annotations being isolated from the rest of the genome.

task_completion.description: Export the extracted tRNA sequences to a multi-FASTA file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An export dialog or file manager/terminal showing the creation of 'human_mt_tRNAs.fasta' in the '~/UGENE_Data/mitochondria/results/' directory.

task_completion.description: Perform a multiple sequence alignment using the MUSCLE algorithm.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A dialog box or alignment view explicitly showing the MUSCLE algorithm being configured or running on the extracted tRNA sequences.

task_completion.description: Export the MUSCLE alignment results to a FASTA file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An export dialog or file manager/terminal showing the creation of 'mt_tRNA_alignment.fasta' in the '~/UGENE_Data/mitochondria/results/' directory.

task_completion.description: Write a summary report containing the total number of tRNAs and the length of the longest tRNA.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal showing the contents of 'tRNA_report.txt' with the total count (22) and the longest length (75 bp).

integrity.description: The agent interacted with a genuine bioinformatics application (like UGENE) to perform the extraction and alignment.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the GUI of a bioinformatics tool being actively used (menus clicked, dialogs opened) rather than just background scripts generating the files.

integrity.description: The MUSCLE alignment was genuinely executed on the data.
integrity.visual_evidence: Presence of the MUSCLE alignment interface, progress bar, or the resulting alignment viewer showing gaps (-) inserted into the sequences.

integrity.description: The agent determined the correct values for the report rather than hallucinating them.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is seen checking the sequence lengths or counts in the GUI or via a command before writing '22' and '75' into the report text file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target genome contains exactly 22 tRNA sequences. The longest extracted tRNA sequence (tRNA-Leu) is exactly 75 base pairs long. The expected output files are 'human_mt_tRNAs.fasta', 'mt_tRNA_alignment.fasta', and 'tRNA_report.txt' located in ~/UGENE_Data/mitochondria/results/.
